papers.title: c-Fos enhances influenza virus replication by stabilizing the M2 protein and promoting autophagosome accumulation
papers.abstract: IAV employs multiple strategies to hijack host cellular machinery. c-Fos identified as critical proviral host factor enhancing IAV replication through stabilizing M2 protein and promoting autophagosome accumulation. IAV infection triggers M2-mediated cytosolic calcium elevation upregulating c-Fos expression. Induced c-Fos physically interacts with M2 preventing its proteasome and lysosomal degradation. Reveals novel positive feedback loop as potential therapeutic target.
papers.relevance_note: Direct host-pathogen molecular interaction: c-Fos/M2 binding, autophagy modulation, positive feedback loop. Very high HPMI relevance with named molecular players.

papers.title: Molecular basis for protection and cross-protection by human antibodies targeting the parainfluenza virus hemagglutinin-neuraminidase protein
papers.abstract: Structural and functional characterization of human antibodies targeting parainfluenza virus HN protein. Cross-protection mechanisms described.
papers.relevance_note: Parainfluenza not influenza. Keyword match on hemagglutinin-neuraminidase but different virus family. Low HPMI relevance for influenza focus.

papers.title: PA-X immunomodulation in the human airway
papers.abstract: Characterizes PA-X endoribonuclease impacts on host response in infected and bystander cells using 3D ex vivo airway epithelium model. PA-X decreases IFN-lambda secretion. Dual activities in evasion of innate and adaptive immune responses. May impact post-infection fibrosis.
papers.relevance_note: Viral immune evasion protein PA-X, host IFN-lambda suppression, 3D airway model. High HPMI relevance with specific molecular mechanism.

papers.title: A novel ISG15 transcript restricts influenza A virus infection
papers.abstract: Novel ISG15 isoform (ncISG15) expressed following IFN stimulation encodes N-terminal truncated protein. Potently inhibits influenza A virus polymerase activity. NS1 reverses ISG15-mediated polymerase inhibition but stoichiometric differences may leave ncISG15 less susceptible. Antiviral activity largely ISGylation-independent.
papers.relevance_note: Host restriction factor ISG15 isoform vs IAV polymerase. NS1 antagonism. Novel mechanism. Very high HPMI relevance.

papers.title: Near real-time data on the human neutralizing antibody landscape to influenza virus as of early 2026 to inform vaccine-strain selection
papers.abstract: High-throughput sequencing-based neutralization assays with 57 H3N2 and 34 H1N1 hemagglutinins. 27,409 titers measured. Lower titers against K subclade H3N2 and D.3.1.1 subclade H1N1.
papers.relevance_note: Influenza serology/vaccine. Relevant to influenza but not molecular host-pathogen interaction mechanisms. Moderate relevance.

papers.title: Bovine-derived influenza A virus (H5N1) shows efficient replication in well-differentiated human nasal epithelial cells without requiring genetic adaptation
papers.abstract: Bovine H5N1 (H5N1Tex/24) replicates as efficiently as 2009 pandemic H1N1 in human nasal epithelial cells. Cells express both avian- and human-type receptors. Replicates effectively at 33C (nasal temperature) unlike earlier avian H5N1.
papers.relevance_note: H5N1 tropism and receptor binding in human cells. Relevant to host-pathogen interface but more epidemiological than molecular mechanism.

papers.title: Antiviral compound targeting host metabolic pathway inhibits H5N1 and H3N2
papers.abstract: Small molecule M4 inhibits replication of H5N1 and H3N2 influenza with IC50 0.09 and 0.12 uM. Partial inhibition of host metabolic checkpoint potentiates direct-acting antivirals.
papers.relevance_note: Host-targeted antiviral. Relevant to host factor role in viral replication. Moderate-high HPMI relevance.

papers.title: Polymerase mutations underlie early adaptation of H5N1 influenza virus to dairy cattle and other mammals
papers.abstract: Cattle B3.13 genotype H5N1 rapidly accumulated polymerase gene adaptations for better replication in bovine cells. PB2 M631L maps to polymerase-ANP32 interface, essential host factor for viral genome replication.
papers.relevance_note: Polymerase-ANP32 host factor interface. Specific molecular interaction at virus-host boundary. High HPMI relevance.
